paper_title: Radiocarbon Dating of the Shroud of Turin

methods.method_name: Robust Statistical Analysis of Radiocarbon Data
methods.description: This method involves applying robust statistical analysis to the configurations of the unknown positions of the 12 official subsamples of the Shroud of Turin to determine the most probable position of each subsample and assess the reliability of the radiocarbon dating results.

steps.description: Apply robust statistical methods to the 387,072 possible configurations of the unknown position of the 12 official subsamples.
steps.searchable_summary: apply robust statistical analysis to subsample configurations
outputs: most probable position of each subsample

steps.description: Determine the most probable position of each subsample and assess the heterogeneity of the data.
steps.searchable_summary: determine subsample positions and assess data heterogeneity

methods.method_name: Correct Sampling Design for Radiocarbon Dating
methods.description: This method outlines a statistically correct sampling design for radiocarbon dating of the Shroud of Turin, aiming to minimize contamination and ensure representative sampling.

steps.description: Consider sampling from several sites of the cloth, focusing on edges to minimize visual impact.
steps.searchable_summary: design sampling strategy focusing on cloth edges

steps.description: Use a space-filling design such as Latin-hypercube to ensure spatial coverage.
steps.searchable_summary: implement Latin-hypercube design for spatial coverage

steps.description: Randomly assign samples to laboratories to avoid spatial bias.
steps.searchable_summary: randomly assign samples to labs to avoid bias

steps.description: Perform a measurement uncertainty study and record possible variables.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct measurement uncertainty study and record variables
